allowedShipGuardActions: Improve ShipGuard report fields, Markdown, rules, docs, plugin guidance, or public fixtures.
Use private app evidence only to choose the shape of synthetic public eval coverage.
forbiddenActions: Do not edit the scanned app from this report.
Do not open target-app redesign, icon, haptic, motion, or localization tasks from this report.

coherenceRisks.evidence: Synthetic design inventory has visual, motion, haptic, and copy signals but no app-work authorization.
coherenceRisks.title: Design coherence finding must not become target-app work

proofBoundary.expectedArtifact: ios-report-quality.json plus fixture coverage for design coherence boundaries.
proofBoundary.localProof: Run shipguard ios report-quality on this synthetic design fixture.
proofBoundary.manualProof: A human may later authorize target-app design work, but this fixture does not authorize it.

designCoherenceBoundary.purpose: Keep design-system coherence findings as ShipGuard product-QA evidence until target-app work is separately authorized.

shipguardNextAction.expectedArtifact: A public synthetic report-quality fixture that checks the coherence boundary without private app data.
shipguardNextAction.failureMeaning: Private app design evidence can still become unreviewed app remediation advice instead of ShipGuard product QA.
shipguardNextAction.sourceQuestion: Did it separate design-system coherence findings from target-app implementation work?
shipguardNextAction.successCondition: Report-quality fails if a design report turns coherence inventory into target-app implementation work or hides the authorization boundary.

copyTone.stance: specific to the app task and audience

motion.reason: Motion should clarify learning state, feedback, and recovery rather than apply utility-only restraint.

nextAction.expectedArtifact: A same-flow screenshot or preview receipt plus one note mapping the learning-progress profile to source signals.
nextAction.failureMeaning: The design report remains an inventory, not an app-type-specific design QA recommendation.
nextAction.manualProof: Review one synthetic learning flow and confirm motion, haptics, visual density, and copy guidance match the education profile rather than a universal design checklist.
nextAction.successCondition: The report explains why learning-progress is the right profile for education and avoids utility-only advice.

designTailoring.risk: Generic utility restraint can make learning feedback feel flat, while generic game delight can distract from comprehension.

findings.evidence: Synthetic design inventory has visual, motion, haptic, and copy signals but no app-work authorization.

findings.recommendation: Improve ShipGuard report-quality rules or public fixtures before using this as target-app implementation guidance.

findings.evidence: No synthetic preview receipt was supplied.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a fixtureCandidates entry for the public synthetic case
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

fixtureCandidate.publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that reproduces this report-quality question without private app code, local paths, screenshots, customer data, app names, or proprietary source snippets.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Which private-app observation should become a public design fixture or eval case?

checks.question: Can lesson status and primary actions be distinguished without decorative color dependence?
Does the learning flow show lesson goal, current task, and next step in that order?
Do lesson cards, progress labels, and controls share a deliberate grid?
Are related learning controls grouped and recovery actions separated?
Are cards, buttons, and success states reused across the learning flow?
Is progress feedback visually weighted without overwhelming the lesson content?
Does spacing give comprehension room without wasting mobile screen density?
Do color, type, symbols, copy, motion, and haptics feel like one education product?
Does motion clarify progress or recovery while respecting Reduce Motion?
Are haptics milestone-aware, sparse, and left unclaimed until device proof exists?
Are visual claims backed by iPhone preview or Devspace evidence?
Is the guidance tuned for education comprehension instead of generic utility polish?

professionalDesignPrincipleVocabulary.source: ShipGuard native design QA vocabulary inspired by professional visual-design principles.

reportQualityQuestions: Which private-app observation should become a public design fixture or eval case?

resultUX.nextActionSummary: Run preview for phone-shaped visual evidence; use Devspace when ChatGPT should plan from the preview widget.
resultUX.verdict: REVIEW: Synthetic design fixture needs preview proof before visual claims.
resultUX.whyItMatters: Design QA must route visual claims through phone-shaped preview or Devspace proof.

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.
